reason: Backend entry point for lxb-core daemon changes.
New backend stores, command handlers, and workflow runner must follow existing module boundaries.
Task templates and workflows introduce new JSON persistence and legacy migration.
New JSON commands must return compact ok/err responses at command boundaries.
Workflow execution must emit trace events for history/debugging.
Command ids, dispatcher, protocol contracts, and backend tests must remain aligned.
Frontend entry point for Android app changes.
Template/workflow UI, parsers, and ViewModel state must fit app layout.
New Templates and Workflows screens must follow Compose component conventions.
User actions that call lxb-core must use ViewModel coroutines and existing gateway patterns.
New template/workflow state belongs in ViewModel/StateFlow and must sync with backend snapshots.
New JSON response shapes need typed parser boundaries and tests.
App/core command contract and parser tests must be kept aligned.
Feature crosses persistence, backend commands, parser, ViewModel, and Compose UI.
Migration should reuse existing schedule, notification, route, and task execution patterns.
